message:
feat(query): implement set operations (UNION, INTERSECT, EXCEPT) (bd-jrd, bd-1h2)

Add set_ops module with:
- SetOpType enum for UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, INTERSECT ALL, EXCEPT, EXCEPT ALL
- SetOperation struct for combining multiple queries with set operations
- Standalone functions: union(), union_all(), intersect(), etc.
- Support for ORDER BY, LIMIT, OFFSET on final result
- Full test coverage with 14 tests

Closes bd-jrd (UNION, UNION ALL) and bd-1h2 (INTERSECT, EXCEPT).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1fz","title":"Define RelationshipInfo struct and RelationshipKind enum","description":"# Task: Define RelationshipInfo and RelationshipKind

## Context
This is the foundational data structure for the entire relationship system. It mirrors Python's RelationshipInfo but adapted for Rust's type system.

## What to Implement

### 1. RelationshipKind Enum
```rust
// In sqlmodel-core/src/relationship.rs (new file)

/// The type of relationship between two models.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum RelationshipKind {
    /// One-to-one: Hero has one Profile
    OneToOne,
    /// Many-to-one: Many Heroes belong to one Team
    ManyToOne,
    /// One-to-many: One Team has many Heroes
    OneToMany,
    /// Many-to-many: Heroes have many Powers, Powers have many Heroes
    ManyToMany,
}
```

### 2. RelationshipInfo Struct
```rust
/// Metadata about a relationship between models.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct RelationshipInfo {
    /// Name of the relationship field
    pub name: &'static str,
    
    /// The related model's table name
    pub related_table: &'static str,
    
    /// Kind of relationship
    pub kind: RelationshipKind,
    
    /// Local foreign key column (for ManyToOne)
    /// e.g., "team_id" on Hero
    pub local_key: Option<&'static str>,
    
    /// Remote foreign key column (for OneToMany)
    /// e.g., "team_id" on Hero when accessed from Team
    pub remote_key: Option<&'static str>,
    
    /// Link table for ManyToMany relationships
    pub link_table: Option<LinkTableInfo>,
    
    /// The field on the related model that points back
    pub back_populates: Option<&'static str>,
    
    /// Whether to use lazy loading
    pub lazy: bool,
    
    /// Cascade delete behavior
    pub cascade_delete: bool,
}
```

### 3. LinkTableInfo for Many-to-Many
```rust
/// Information about a link/join table for many-to-many relationships.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LinkTableInfo {
    /// The link table name (e.g., "hero_powers")
    pub table_name: &'static str,
    
    /// Column in link table pointing to local model
    /// e.g., "hero_id" in hero_powers
    pub local_column: &'static str,
    
    /// Column in link table pointing to remote model
    /// e.g., "power_id" in hero_powers
    pub remote_column: &'static str,
}
```

### 4. Add to Model Trait
```rust
// Extend the Model trait
pub trait Model: Sized {
    // ... existing methods ...
    
    /// Relationship metadata for this model
    const RELATIONSHIPS: &'static [RelationshipInfo] = &[];
}
```

## Files to Modify
- Create: `crates/sqlmodel-core/src/relationship.rs`
- Modify: `crates/sqlmodel-core/src/lib.rs` (export new types)
- Modify: `crates/sqlmodel-core/src/model.rs` (add RELATIONSHIPS to trait)

## Testing
- Unit tests for RelationshipKind methods
- Unit tests for RelationshipInfo builder pattern
- Ensure Default impl exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RelationshipKind enum with all 4 variants
- [ ] RelationshipInfo struct with all fields
- [ ] LinkTableInfo struct
- [ ] Builder pattern for RelationshipInfo (const fn where possible)
- [ ] Model trait updated with RELATIONSHIPS constant
- [ ] All types exported from sqlmodel-core
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] Documentation with examples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:14:40.670335947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:39:38.877211592Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:39:38.877144336Z","close_reason":"Completed: RelationshipKind/Info + Model::RELATIONSHIPS","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fz","depends_on_id":"bd-1ak","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:14:40.688123478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ga","title":"Implement multiple_of validation constraint","description":"## Description

Add validation that numeric value must be multiple of specified value.

## Python Behavior

```python
class Product(SQLModel):
    quantity: int = Field(multiple_of=5)  # Must be 0, 5, 10, 15...
    price: Decimal = Field(multiple_of=0.01)  # Cents only
```

## Rust Implementation

### Macro Attribute
```rust
#[derive(Model, Validate)]
struct Product {
    #[validate(multiple_of = 5)]
    quantity: i32,
    
    #[validate(multiple_of = 0.01)]
    price: Decimal,
}
```

### Generated Validation Code
```rust
if self.quantity % 5 != 0 {
    errors.add_multiple_of("quantity", 5, self.quantity);
}
```

## Files to Modify

- crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/validate_derive.rs
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/error.rs (add ValidationError variant)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] multiple_of works with integers
- [ ] multiple_of works with floats/decimals
- [ ] Clear error message
- [ ] Works with negative numbers

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/validate_derive.rs)
- [ ] Test multiple_of validation for integers
- [ ] Test multiple_of validation for floats
- [ ] Test edge cases (0, negative)
- [ ] Test with other numeric constraints

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/multiple_of_validation.rs)
- [ ] value with multiple_of=5 accepts 10, 15
- [ ] Rejects 7 when multiple_of=5
- [ ] Float multiple_of=0.5
- [ ] Combined with min/max constraints
- [ ] Error message includes constraint

### Logging
- [ ] TRACE: Multiple of check

 {"id":"bd-1gn","title":"Advanced Type Mappings","description":"## Overview

Implement ALL Python type to SQL type mappings from SQLModel.

## Required Type Mappings

### Basic Types (✅ Implemented)
- str → VARCHAR/TEXT
- int → INTEGER
- float → FLOAT/REAL
- bool → BOOLEAN

### Date/Time Types (Partial)
- datetime → TIMESTAMP/DATETIME
- date → DATE
- time → TIME
- timedelta → INTERVAL

### Numeric Types (Gaps)
- Decimal → NUMERIC(precision, scale)
  - Must respect max_digits and decimal_places from Field()
  - Currently using workaround with sql_type attribute

### Binary Types
- bytes → BLOB/BYTEA

### Special Types
- UUID → UUID (Postgres) / CHAR(36) (MySQL/SQLite)
- Enum → ENUM or VARCHAR with CHECK constraint
- JSON → JSON/JSONB
- ARRAY → ARRAY (Postgres only)

### Network Types (Python-specific, need Rust equivalents)
- ipaddress.IPv4Address → VARCHAR/INET
- ipaddress.IPv4Network → VARCHAR/CIDR
- ipaddress.IPv6Address → VARCHAR/INET
- ipaddress.IPv6Network → VARCHAR/CIDR

### Path Types
- pathlib.Path → VARCHAR

### Pydantic Types
- EmailStr → VARCHAR (with validation)
- HttpUrl → VARCHAR (with validation)
- AnyUrl → VARCHAR
- SecretStr → VARCHAR
- PositiveInt → INTEGER (with CHECK)
- NegativeInt → INTEGER (with CHECK)
- PositiveFloat → FLOAT (with CHECK)
- NegativeFloat → FLOAT (with CHECK)

## Dialect-Specific Handling

Each type must generate correct SQL for:
- PostgreSQL
- MySQL
- SQLite

With appropriate fallbacks when type not natively supported.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:01:04.215337447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:58:25.441344856Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gn","depends_on_id":"bd-162","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:58:25.441316494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1gp","title":"Implement Session.add_all() for bulk add","description":"## Description

Add multiple objects to session at once.

## Python Behavior

```python
session.add_all([user1, user2, user3])
```

Equivalent to calling add() for each, but potentially more efficient.

## Rust Implementation

```rust
impl Session {
    pub fn add_all<M: Model>(&mut self, models: impl IntoIterator<Item = M>) {
        for model in models {
            self.add(model);
        }
    }
}
```

May also want variant that takes references:
```rust
pub fn add_all_ref<M: Model>(&mut self, models: &[M]) { ... }
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] add_all works with Vec, slice, iterator
- [ ] All objects tracked in session
- [ ] Efficient (single transaction on flush)
- [ ] Tests for edge cases (empty list, mixed types)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-core/src/session/add.rs)
- [ ] Test add_all with Vec
- [ ] Test add_all with empty collection
- [ ] Test add_all cascades to relationships
- [ ] Test add_all with mixed new/existing
- [ ] Test add_all performance

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/session_add_all.rs)
- [ ] add_all([obj1, obj2, obj3]) → all tracked
- [ ] add_all with relationships
- [ ] add_all → flush → all in DB
- [ ] Large batch add_all performance
- [ ] add_all with duplicates

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Objects added count
- [ ] TRACE: Individual object tracking

-{"id":"bd-1h2","title":"Implement INTERSECT, EXCEPT set operations","description":"## Description

Support INTERSECT and EXCEPT to combine query results.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlalchemy import intersect, intersect_all, except_, except_all

# INTERSECT - rows in both queries
stmt = intersect(query1, query2)
stmt = intersect_all(query1, query2)  # Keep duplicates

# EXCEPT - rows in first but not second
stmt = except_(query1, query2)
stmt = except_all(query1, query2)
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
let active_admins = select!(User)
    .filter(User::role.eq("admin"))
    .intersect(
        select!(User).filter(User::active.eq(true))
    );

let non_admins = select!(User)
    .except(
        select!(User).filter(User::role.eq("admin"))
    );
```

## SQLite Note

SQLite supports INTERSECT and EXCEPT but not the ALL variants.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] intersect() method implemented
- [ ] intersect_all() method implemented
- [ ] except_() method implemented
- [ ] except_all() method implemented
- [ ] Dialect-aware SQL generation
- [ ] Error on unsupported operations (SQLite ALL)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-query/src/set_ops.rs)
- [ ] Test INTERSECT generation
- [ ] Test EXCEPT generation
- [ ] Test INTERSECT ALL
- [ ] Test EXCEPT ALL
- [ ] Test combining with ORDER BY

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/query_set_ops.rs)
- [ ] INTERSECT returns common rows
- [ ] EXCEPT returns difference
- [ ] INTERSECT ALL keeps duplicates
- [ ] Chain: A INTERSECT B EXCEPT C
- [ ] Set ops with different column types

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Set operation SQL generation

 {"id":"bd-1hud","title":"Implement model_config equivalent","description":"## Description

Support model-level configuration matching Pydantic's model_config.

## Python Behavior

```python
class User(SQLModel, table=True):
    model_config = ConfigDict(
        table=True,
        from_attributes=True,
        validate_assignment=True,
        extra='forbid',
        strict=True,
        populate_by_name=True,
        use_enum_values=True,
    )
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
#[derive(Model)]
#[sqlmodel(
    table,
    from_attributes,
    validate_assignment,
    extra = "forbid",
    strict,
    populate_by_name,
    use_enum_values
)]
struct User {
    // ...
}
```

## Config Options

- **table**: Create database table
- **from_attributes**: Read from object attributes
- **validate_assignment**: Validate when setting attributes
- **extra**: 'allow', 'forbid', 'ignore' extra fields
- **strict**: Strict type checking
- **populate_by_name**: Allow both name and alias
- **use_enum_values**: Use enum values not names
- **arbitrary_types_allowed**: Allow non-standard types

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All config options parseable
- [ ] Affect behavior correctly
- [ ] Inheritable from base models
- [ ] Overridable in submodels

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/model.rs)
- [ ] Test all config options parsed
- [ ] Test config affects behavior
- [ ] Test config inheritance
- [ ] Test config override in subclass

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/model_config.rs)
- [ ] table=True creates table
- [ ] from_attributes=True enables ORM mode
- [ ] validate_assignment=True validates on set
- [ ] extra="forbid" rejects extra fields
- [ ] Config inheritance from base model

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Model configuration applied
- [ ] TRACE: Individual config options

 {"id":"bd-1jy","title":"Implement N+1 query detection","description":"# Task: Implement N+1 Query Detection

## Context
The N+1 query problem occurs when code loads N objects and then lazily loads a relationship for each, resulting in N+1 database queries instead of 2. This task implements detection and warning for this anti-pattern.

## Detection Strategy
1. **Query counting per relationship**: Track queries by (model, relationship) pair
2. **Threshold-based warning**: Warn when queries exceed threshold (default: 3)
3. **Stack trace capture**: Show where lazy loads originate
4. **Suggestion generation**: Recommend batch loading alternative

## What to Implement

### 1. Query Tracker
\`\`\`rust
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};

/// Tracks lazy load queries for N+1 detection.
#[derive(Debug, Default)]
pub struct N1QueryTracker {
    /// (parent_type, relationship_name) -> query count
    counts: HashMap<(&'static str, &'static str), AtomicUsize>,
    /// Threshold for warning (queries per relationship)
    threshold: usize,
    /// Whether detection is enabled
    enabled: bool,
    /// Captured call sites for debugging
    call_sites: Vec<CallSite>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct CallSite {
    pub parent_type: &'static str,
    pub relationship: &'static str,
    pub file: &'static str,
    pub line: u32,
    pub timestamp: std::time::Instant,
}

impl N1QueryTracker {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            counts: HashMap::new(),
            threshold: 3,
            enabled: true,
            call_sites: Vec::new(),
        }
    }
    
    pub fn with_threshold(mut self, threshold: usize) -> Self {
        self.threshold = threshold;
        self
    }
    
    pub fn disable(&mut self) {
        self.enabled = false;
    }
    
    /// Record a lazy load query.
    #[track_caller]
    pub fn record_load(&mut self, parent_type: &'static str, relationship: &'static str) {
        if !self.enabled { return; }
        
        let key = (parent_type, relationship);
        let count = self.counts
            .entry(key)
            .or_insert_with(|| AtomicUsize::new(0))
            .fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed) + 1;
        
        // Capture call site
        let caller = std::panic::Location::caller();
        self.call_sites.push(CallSite {
            parent_type,
            relationship,
            file: caller.file(),
            line: caller.line(),
            timestamp: std::time::Instant::now(),
        });
        
        // Check threshold
        if count == self.threshold {
            self.emit_warning(parent_type, relationship, count);
        }
    }
    
    fn emit_warning(&self, parent_type: &'static str, relationship: &'static str, count: usize) {
        tracing::warn!(
            parent = parent_type,
            relationship = relationship,
            queries = count,
            threshold = self.threshold,
            "N+1 query pattern detected! Consider using Session::load_many() for batch loading."
        );
        
        // Log recent call sites for this relationship
        let sites: Vec<_> = self.call_sites.iter()
            .filter(|s| s.parent_type == parent_type && s.relationship == relationship)
            .take(5)
            .collect();
        
        for site in sites {
            tracing::warn!(
                file = site.file,
                line = site.line,
                "  -> Lazy load at {}:{}",
                site.file,
                site.line
            );
        }
    }
    
    /// Reset counts (call at start of request/transaction).
    pub fn reset(&mut self) {
        self.counts.clear();
        self.call_sites.clear();
    }
    
    /// Get current statistics.
    pub fn stats(&self) -> N1Stats {
        N1Stats {
            total_loads: self.counts.values()
                .map(|c| c.load(Ordering::Relaxed))
                .sum(),
            relationships_loaded: self.counts.len(),
            potential_n1: self.counts.iter()
                .filter(|(_, c)| c.load(Ordering::Relaxed) >= self.threshold)
                .count(),
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct N1Stats {
    pub total_loads: usize,
    pub relationships_loaded: usize,
    pub potential_n1: usize,
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Integration with Lazy<T>
\`\`\`rust
impl<T: Model> Lazy<T> {
    pub async fn load(&mut self, session: &mut Session) -> Result<Option<&T>> {
        // Record for N+1 detection
        if let Some(tracker) = session.n1_tracker() {
            tracker.record_load(
                std::any::type_name::<Self>(), // Parent type would need to be passed
                "lazy_relationship", // Would need relationship name
            );
        }
        
        // ... existing load logic ...
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Session Integration
\`\`\`rust
impl Session {
    pub fn enable_n1_detection(&mut self, threshold: usize) {
        self.n1_tracker = Some(N1QueryTracker::new().with_threshold(threshold));
    }
    
    pub fn n1_tracker(&mut self) -> Option<&mut N1QueryTracker> {
        self.n1_tracker.as_mut()
    }
    
    pub fn n1_stats(&self) -> Option<N1Stats> {
        self.n1_tracker.as_ref().map(|t| t.stats())
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 4. Middleware/Scope Pattern
\`\`\`rust
/// RAII guard for N+1 detection scope.
pub struct N1DetectionScope<'s> {
    session: &'s mut Session,
}

impl<'s> N1DetectionScope<'s> {
    pub fn new(session: &'s mut Session, threshold: usize) -> Self {
        session.enable_n1_detection(threshold);
        Self { session }
    }
}

impl Drop for N1DetectionScope<'_> {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if let Some(stats) = self.session.n1_stats() {
            if stats.potential_n1 > 0 {
                tracing::warn!(
                    potential_n1 = stats.potential_n1,
                    total_loads = stats.total_loads,
                    "N+1 issues detected in this scope"
                );
            }
        }
        self.session.n1_tracker = None;
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Files to Modify
- Create: \`crates/sqlmodel-session/src/n1_detection.rs\`
- Modify: \`crates/sqlmodel-session/src/session.rs\`
- Modify: \`crates/sqlmodel-core/src/relationship.rs\` (Lazy<T>)

## Dependencies
- Lazy<T> wrapper (bd-1q3)
- Session struct (bd-qv5)

---

## Comprehensive Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

1. **N1QueryTracker Tests**
   - \`test_tracker_records_single_load\`: Count increments on record_load
   - \`test_tracker_records_multiple_relationships\`: Separate counts per relationship
   - \`test_tracker_threshold_triggers_warning\`: Warning at threshold
   - \`test_tracker_below_threshold_no_warning\`: No warning below threshold
   - \`test_tracker_disabled_no_recording\`: Disabled tracker does nothing
   - \`test_tracker_reset_clears_counts\`: reset() zeros everything

2. **CallSite Capture Tests**
   - \`test_callsite_captures_location\`: File and line recorded
   - \`test_callsite_timestamp_monotonic\`: Later calls have later timestamps

3. **Stats Tests**
   - \`test_stats_total_loads_accurate\`: Sum of all counts
   - \`test_stats_relationships_count\`: Distinct relationships
   - \`test_stats_potential_n1_count\`: Only those over threshold

4. **Scope Tests**
   - \`test_scope_enables_detection\`: N1DetectionScope enables tracker
   - \`test_scope_drop_logs_summary\`: Drop logs stats if issues found
   - \`test_scope_drop_clears_tracker\`: Tracker removed after drop

### Integration Tests

1. **With Session**
   - \`test_session_n1_detection_flag\`: enable_n1_detection() works
   - \`test_session_lazy_load_recorded\`: Lazy<T>.load() calls record_load
   - \`test_session_batch_load_not_recorded\`: load_many() doesn't trigger N+1

2. **Real Scenario**
   - \`test_n1_detected_in_loop\`: for hero in heroes { hero.team.load() } triggers warning
   - \`test_batch_load_avoids_n1\`: load_many() keeps count at 1

### E2E Test Script

\`\`\`rust
/// E2E: N+1 detection warns appropriately
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_n1_detection_in_loop() {
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    // Enable N+1 detection with low threshold
    session.enable_n1_detection(3);
    
    // Setup: Create 10 heroes with teams
    setup_heroes_with_teams(&pool, 10).await;
    
    // Load heroes
    let mut heroes = session.query::<Hero>().all().await.unwrap();
    
    // Lazy load in loop - should trigger N+1 warning
    for hero in &mut heroes {
        hero.team.load(&mut session).await.unwrap();
    }
    
    // Check stats
    let stats = session.n1_stats().unwrap();
    assert!(stats.potential_n1 > 0, "Should detect N+1 pattern");
    tracing::warn!(stats = ?stats, "N+1 stats after loop");
}

/// E2E: Batch loading avoids N+1
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_batch_load_no_n1() {
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    session.enable_n1_detection(3);
    
    setup_heroes_with_teams(&pool, 10).await;
    
    let mut heroes = session.query::<Hero>().all().await.unwrap();
    
    // Batch load - should NOT trigger N+1
    session.load_many(&mut heroes, |h| &mut h.team).await.unwrap();
    
    let stats = session.n1_stats().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(stats.potential_n1, 0, "Batch load should not trigger N+1");
    tracing::info!(stats = ?stats, "N+1 stats after batch load");
}

/// E2E: N1DetectionScope usage
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_detection_scope() {
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    {
        let _scope = N1DetectionScope::new(&mut session, 2);
        
        // Do work that might cause N+1
        // ...
        
        // Scope drop will log if issues found
    }
    
    // Tracker cleared after scope
    assert!(session.n1_stats().is_none());
}
\`\`\`

### Logging Requirements

\`\`\`rust
impl N1QueryTracker {
    fn emit_warning(&self, parent_type: &'static str, relationship: &'static str, count: usize) {
        tracing::warn!(
            target: "sqlmodel::n1",
            parent = parent_type,
            relationship = relationship,
            queries = count,
            threshold = self.threshold,
            suggestion = "Use Session::load_many() for batch loading",
            "N+1 QUERY PATTERN DETECTED"
        );
        
        // Detailed call site logging
        tracing::debug!(
            target: "sqlmodel::n1",
            "Call sites for this N+1:"
        );
        for (i, site) in self.call_sites.iter()
            .filter(|s| s.parent_type == parent_type && s.relationship == relationship)
            .enumerate()
        {
            tracing::debug!(
                target: "sqlmodel::n1",
                index = i,
                file = site.file,
                line = site.line,
                "  [{}] {}:{}",
                i,
                site.file,
                site.line
            );
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] N1QueryTracker records lazy loads
- [ ] Threshold-based warning emission
- [ ] Call site capture with file:line
- [ ] Stats reporting (total, relationships, potential N+1)
- [ ] Session integration (enable_n1_detection, n1_stats)
- [ ] N1DetectionScope RAII helper
- [ ] Suggestion in warning message
- [ ] Tracing at warn/debug levels
- [ ] Unit tests: 12+ test cases
- [ ] Integration tests: 4+ tests
- [ ] E2E tests: 3 workflow tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:26:09.106809395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:09:52.348979012Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:09:52.348823983Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jy","depends_on_id":"bd-1q3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:28:47.687818942Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jy","depends_on_id":"bd-1qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:26:09.116336183Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-1jy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented N+1 query detection:

Implemented N+1 query detection with the following components:

### Core Implementation
- `N1QueryTracker` - Tracks lazy load queries per (parent_type, relationship) pair
- `CallSite` - Captures location info (file, line, timestamp) for debugging
- `N1Stats` - Statistics about detection (total_loads, relationships_loaded, potential_n1)
- `N1DetectionScope` - RAII guard that captures initial stats and logs summary on drop

### Features
- Configurable threshold (default: 3 queries triggers warning)
- Enable/disable detection at runtime
- Reset counts between request/transaction scopes
- Automatic warning emission via tracing when threshold reached
- Call site tracking for debugging N+1 sources

### Tests
All 26 unit tests passing in n1_detection.rs covering:
- Tracker defaults and configuration
- Recording and counting loads
- Multiple relationships tracking
- Enable/disable behavior
- Reset functionality
- Call site capture and timestamps
- Stats accuracy
- N1DetectionScope RAII behavior

### Usage Example
```rust
// Enable detection
session.enable_n1_detection(3);

// RAII scope for automatic summary
{
    let _scope = N1DetectionScope::from_tracker(session.n1_tracker());
    
    // This triggers warning after 3rd load:
    for hero in &mut heroes {
        hero.team.load(&mut session).await?;
    }
} // Logs summary on drop

// Fix: use batch loading
session.load_many(&mut heroes, |h| &mut h.team).await?;
```

 {"id":"bd-1lh","title":"Implement BatchOperationTracker for bulk database operations","description":"## Purpose
Create a specialized tracker for bulk database operations that tracks batches, rows affected, errors, and calculates rates automatically.

## Background
Bulk operations like batch inserts, updates, or data migrations have specific tracking needs:
- Batch-level progress (batch 5 of 20)
- Row-level progress (2500 rows inserted)
- Error counting and threshold warnings
- Automatic rate calculation
- Summary statistics at completion

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/batch_tracker.rs

### Core Struct
BatchOperationTracker holds:
- operation_name: String
- total_batches: u64
- completed_batches: u64
- total_rows: u64
- processed_rows: u64
- error_count: u64
- started_at: Instant
- batch_times: Vec<Duration> (for rate smoothing)
- theme: Theme

### Rendering (Rich Mode)
Two-line display:
Line 1: Batch progress bar
Line 2: Row count, rate, errors

Batch insert [=========>        ] 50% (10/20 batches)
  Rows: 5,000/10,000 | Rate: 523 rows/s | Errors: 0

### Rate Calculation
- Track last N batch durations for smoothed rate
- rows_per_second = rows_in_recent_batches / duration_of_recent_batches
- Avoids spiky rates from single fast/slow batches

### Error Highlighting
- 0 errors: green or hidden
- 1+ errors: yellow with count
- Errors > threshold (configurable): red with warning

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
Batch insert: 50% (10/20 batches), 5000/10000 rows, 523 rows/s, 0 errors

### Completion Summary
When all batches complete, show summary panel:
- Total time
- Total rows
- Average rate
- Error count
- Success rate percentage

## API Design
BatchOperationTracker::new(name, total_batches, total_rows)
  .theme(theme)
  .error_threshold(10)  // warn when errors exceed this
  .complete_batch(rows_in_batch)
  .record_error()
  .render(width) / .render_plain()
  .render_summary()  // final summary

## Verification Steps
1. Test normal batch completion
2. Test with errors
3. Test error threshold warning
4. Verify rate calculation accuracy
5. Test completion summary
6. Verify plain text format
7. Test with single batch (edge case)

## Dependencies
- OperationProgress (reuse progress bar logic)
- Theme from this crate
- std::time for timing","acceptance_criteria":"BatchOperationTracker shows overall batch progress
Tracker shows individual item status within batch
Tracker handles cancellation gracefully
Plain mode outputs batch status updates
All unit tests verify batch tracking
Performance tests confirm acceptable overhead for large batches","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:52.556586848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:28:47.633125588Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:28:47.633082677Z","close_reason":"Implemented BatchOperationTracker with rate smoothing, error tracking, and 26 unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lh","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1lh","depends_on_id":"bd-3gj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-1lh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_batch_tracker_creation - verify initial state
2. test_batch_complete - verify batch counting
3. test_batch_rows_tracking - verify row accumulation
4. test_batch_rate_calculation - verify throughput
5. test_batch_error_recording - verify error count
6. test_batch_error_threshold - verify warning trigger
7. test_batch_render_plain - verify text format
8. test_batch_summary - verify completion summary
9. test_batch_single_batch - edge case
10. test_batch_many_batches - stress test","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-iy1","title":"Implement collection validators (min_items, max_items, unique_items)","description":"## Description

Add validation for collection (Vec, array) fields.

## Python Behavior

```python
class Order(SQLModel):
    items: List[str] = Field(
        min_items=1,        # At least 1 item
        max_items=100,      # At most 100 items
        unique_items=True   # No duplicates
    )
```

## Rust Implementation

### Macro Attributes
```rust
#[derive(Model, Validate)]
struct Order {
    #[validate(min_items = 1, max_items = 100, unique_items)]
    items: Vec<String>,
}
```

### Generated Validation
```rust
if self.items.len() < 1 {
    errors.add_min_items("items", 1, self.items.len());
}
if self.items.len() > 100 {
    errors.add_max_items("items", 100, self.items.len());
}
if self.items.len() != self.items.iter().collect::<HashSet<_>>().len() {
    errors.add_unique_items("items");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] min_items enforces minimum collection size
- [ ] max_items enforces maximum collection size
- [ ] unique_items ensures no duplicates
- [ ] Works with Vec, HashSet, arrays
- [ ] Clear error messages

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/validate_derive.rs)
- [ ] Test min_items validation
- [ ] Test max_items validation
- [ ] Test unique_items validation
- [ ] Test combined constraints

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/collection_validators.rs)
- [ ] Vec with min_items=1 rejects empty
- [ ] Vec with max_items=10 rejects 11
- [ ] unique_items rejects duplicates
- [ ] All constraints on same field
- [ ] Nested collection validation

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Collection validation
- [ ] TRACE: Item count checking

-{"id":"bd-jrd","title":"Implement UNION, UNION ALL set operations","description":"## Description

Support UNION and UNION ALL to combine query results.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlalchemy import union, union_all

# UNION (removes duplicates)
stmt = union(
    select(User).where(User.type == 'admin'),
    select(User).where(User.type == 'superuser')
)

# UNION ALL (keeps duplicates)
stmt = union_all(query1, query2, query3)

result = session.exec(stmt)
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
// Method chaining
let query = select!(User)
    .filter(User::type_.eq("admin"))
    .union(
        select!(User).filter(User::type_.eq("superuser"))
    );

// Function style
let query = union([query1, query2, query3]);
let query = union_all([query1, query2]);
```

### SQL Generation

```sql
(SELECT ... FROM users WHERE type = 'admin')
UNION
(SELECT ... FROM users WHERE type = 'superuser')
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] union() method on Select
- [ ] union_all() method on Select
- [ ] Standalone union(), union_all() functions
- [ ] Works with different SELECT columns (must match)
- [ ] Correct SQL generation for all dialects
- [ ] Can chain multiple unions

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-query/src/set_ops.rs)
- [ ] Test UNION generation
- [ ] Test UNION ALL generation
- [ ] Test column compatibility check
- [ ] Test with ORDER BY/LIMIT

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/query_union.rs)
- [ ] UNION removes duplicates
- [ ] UNION ALL keeps duplicates
- [ ] UNION with different queries
- [ ] UNION with ORDER BY at end
- [ ] UNION subquery in FROM

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: UNION clause SQL generation

 {"id":"bd-kgpu","title":"Implement bulk operations (bulk_insert, bulk_update)","description":"## Description

Efficient bulk insert/update without individual object tracking.

## Python Behavior

```python
# Bulk insert without object tracking
session.bulk_insert_mappings(User, [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
])

# Bulk update
session.bulk_update_mappings(User, [
    {'id': 1, 'name': 'Alice Updated'},
    {'id': 2, 'name': 'Bob Updated'},
])

# Bulk save objects
session.bulk_save_objects([user1, user2, user3])
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
impl Session {
    pub async fn bulk_insert<M: Model>(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        models: &[M],
    ) -> Outcome<u64, Error> {
        // Single INSERT with multiple rows
        // INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES 
        //   ('Alice', 'alice@...'),
        //   ('Bob', 'bob@...')
    }
    
    pub async fn bulk_update<M: Model>(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        models: &[M],
    ) -> Outcome<u64, Error> {
        // Use CASE WHEN for batch update or multiple statements
    }
}
```

### Optimizations

- Batch into chunks (avoid huge queries)
- Use COPY for PostgreSQL bulk load
- Use LOAD DATA for MySQL

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] bulk_insert with efficient SQL
- [ ] bulk_update with efficient SQL
- [ ] Batch size configurable
- [ ] Returns affected row count
- [ ] Skips object tracking overhead

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-core/src/bulk.rs)
- [ ] Test bulk_insert generates multi-value INSERT
- [ ] Test bulk_update generates CASE/WHEN or multi-UPDATE
- [ ] Test batch size configuration
- [ ] Test empty collection handling
- [ ] Test large batch chunking

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/bulk_operations.rs)
- [ ] bulk_insert 1000 records → single statement
- [ ] bulk_update 1000 records efficiently
- [ ] bulk_insert with returning IDs
- [ ] bulk_insert with FK validation
- [ ] Performance vs individual inserts (10x+ faster)

### Logging
- [ ] INFO: Bulk operation size
- [ ] DEBUG: Batch chunking details
- [ ] TRACE: Individual record processing

 {"id":"bd-kzp1","title":"Implement table inheritance patterns","description":"## Description

Support SQLAlchemy-style table inheritance.

## Single Table Inheritance

All subclasses in one table with discriminator:
```python
class Employee(SQLModel, table=True):
    type: str
    
class Manager(Employee):
    department: str  # Same table, type='manager'
    
class Engineer(Employee):
    specialty: str  # Same table, type='engineer'
```

## Joined Table Inheritance

Each class has own table, FKs link them:
```python
class Person(SQLModel, table=True):
    id: int
    name: str

class Employee(Person, table=True):
    employee_id: int  # FK to person.id
    department: str
```

## Concrete Table Inheritance

Each class is independent table:
```python
class ConcreteEmployee(SQLModel, table=True):
    # All fields repeated, no inheritance at DB level
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
// Single table
#[derive(Model)]
#[sqlmodel(table, inheritance = "single", discriminator = "type")]
struct Employee {
    type_: String,
}

#[derive(Model)]
#[sqlmodel(inherits = "Employee", discriminator_value = "manager")]
struct Manager {
    department: String,
}

// Joined
#[derive(Model)]
#[sqlmodel(table, inheritance = "joined")]
struct Person { ... }

#[derive(Model)]
#[sqlmodel(table, inherits = "Person")]
struct Employee { ... }
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single table inheritance
- [ ] Joined table inheritance
- [ ] Concrete table inheritance
- [ ] Discriminator column support
- [ ] Polymorphic queries

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/inheritance.rs)
- [ ] Test single table inheritance
- [ ] Test joined table inheritance
- [ ] Test concrete table inheritance
- [ ] Test discriminator column
- [ ] Test polymorphic queries

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/table_inheritance.rs)
- [ ] Single table: base + derived in same table
- [ ] Joined table: base in one, derived in another with FK
- [ ] Concrete table: each class has own table
- [ ] Query base class returns all subclasses
- [ ] Discriminator column populated correctly

### Logging
